# gcore_storage_object_storage_bucket (Data Source)

Buckets are containers within object storage that hold files (objects) and define their CORS, lifecycle, and access policy configuration.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "gcore_storage_object_storage_bucket" "example_storage_object_storage_bucket" {
  storage_id = 0
  name = "name"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String)
- `storage_id` (Number)

### Read-Only

- `cors` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--cors))
- `policy` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--policy))
- `storage_object_storage_bucket_lifecycle` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--storage_object_storage_bucket_lifecycle))

<a id="nestedatt--cors"></a>
### Nested Schema for `cors`

Read-Only:

- `allowed_origins` (List of String) Web domains allowed to make direct browser requests to this bucket (e.g., "https://myapp.com"). Use "*" to allow any origin.


<a id="nestedatt--policy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `policy`

Read-Only:

- `is_public` (Boolean) When true, anyone can download objects without credentials. When false, all requests require valid S3 authentication.


<a id="nestedatt--storage_object_storage_bucket_lifecycle"></a>
### Nested Schema for `storage_object_storage_bucket_lifecycle`

Read-Only:

- `expiration_days` (Number) Days after upload before objects are automatically deleted. For example, 30 means files are removed 30 days after creation.
